_authoring_notes: v3 first-pass rubric (no prior v2 on main). Authored via the scaleout author↔Gemini-critic loop under the strict-provenance bar: every scored goal must trace to a verbatim or paraphrased user message (instruction.md, user_simulation_prompt.md, or oracle_intents.json). Oracle-specific identifiers the user never voiced are stripped unless the user pinned them verbatim. Weights reflect user emphasis, not test_manifest.yaml F2P gate weights.

_provenance.goal_0: N/A — anti-gaming guard, derived from the harbor benchmark's defensive floor (no positive weight, can only zero the trial). Not a user ask.
_provenance.goal_1: Turn-0 verbatim: 'when I click into a travel segment from within the TasksPane, I want it to open within the context of the shot it's a part of - similar to how it would when i open via SegmentOutputStrip ... so that i can click back/forth to previous segments'. Also U5: 'it doesn't show the chevron, the buttons/links to the constituent images don't show/work ... opening it outside of the context of that shot.' The 'genuine task-type field' clause encodes the root-cause bug (detection predicate hit the wrong field) behaviorally without naming the identifier; the three real model patches that only partially wired this scored 0.1.
_provenance.goal_2: U5 debug logs show the failure mode the user reported: 'shotId: undefined ... segmentSlotsCount: 0' — the context arrived empty so chevrons/constituent images were blank. The user's Turn-0 goal ('click back/forth to previous segments') is only met if the shot/segment identity is correctly propagated to the receiver. Behavioral phrasing ('shot context carried through', 'non-empty') avoids naming the openSegmentSlot/shotId identifiers, which the user did not state verbatim.
_provenance.goal_3: U7 verbatim (Turn 3 deliverable): 'when i clicked it it first showed the empty form - the one that shows when there's no video for a segment - before switching out to the proper media lightbox'. Multi-turn correction. Phrased behaviorally (wait for data availability) rather than naming the segmentSlots/pairDataByIndex effect.
_provenance.goal_4: U9 verbatim: 'What about in cases where the video exists but it wouldn't actually display on the shot because that segment or position has been deleted? Can we fall back to the old-school viewer?' plus U10 'Let's do A' (chose the pre-check approach), U11 'Now it seems to be showing simple for all' (branch collapsed to one path), and the later stacking report: 'i first click into one that belongs to a shot and it enters it then i click into another one that doesn't belong ... then when i try to click back the other one it just doesn't work. However, when i close that and then click it does' (the open viewer blocked the next open). The 'distinct branch / not all-one-way' and 'must not stay open and block' clauses encode the U11 and stacking corrections behaviorally.
_provenance.goal_5: Second-half multi-turn arc (Turns 18-24). U18 verbatim: 'Why when I clear the prompt does enhance prompt get set to true?'; U19: 'It should always be set to false and nothing should ever set it to true ... find every instance of it ... It should only be true if it's persisting the user's existing settings.'; U21: 'Except on the batch generator, that's the one place you should default to true.' U19's 'It should only be true if it's persisting the user's existing settings' is the verbatim basis for the preserve-on-clear clause: the proximate bug wiped the saved preference on clear, so a fix that just flips the default off would silently lose a user's enabled setting. Behavioral phrasing avoids naming the enhancePrompt/enhance_prompt_enabled identifiers; the 'every default path' clause encodes the user's DRY/'tons of different places' complaint as the observable requirement (no path leaks an on-default). The thin U25 'make a .md doc' ask is a process/deliverable-format request, not a behavioral workspace outcome, so it is intentionally not given its own scored goal.

_dropped_from_v2.note: No v2 rubric existed on main for this task. This is the first-pass rubric authored at the v3 bar via the scaleout author↔critic loop.

_added_for_coverage.note: Not applicable — no v2 baseline to diff against. Per-goal user-ask tracing lives in each goal's rationale and in _provenance.

_self_critique_log: goal_0: anti_gaming floor present, weight 0.0 — OK.
goal_1: weight=0.4, tier=core, user-turn cite=see rationale — provenance OK.
goal_2: weight=0.2, tier=core, user-turn cite=see rationale — provenance OK.
goal_3: weight=0.13, tier=core, user-turn cite=yes — provenance OK.
goal_4: weight=0.15, tier=core, user-turn cite=see rationale — provenance OK.
goal_5: weight=0.12, tier=core, user-turn cite=see rationale — provenance OK.
Gemini critic final: verdict=PASS score=1.0 round=3.
strength: Exceptional behavioral phrasing in Goal 1 that perfectly captures the root cause of the `taskType` vs `task_type` bug without naming the identifiers, allowing diverse valid implementations.
strength: Excellent handling of the multi-turn stacking and orphaned-segment fallback requirements, explicitly permitting both in-place and navigation-based lightbox architectures.
strength: Watertight anti-gaming clauses in Goal 5 that specifically prevent a partial fix from silently wiping the user's saved preferences.

residual risk: An LLM judge might misinterpret 'distinct fallback' in Goal 4 to require two completely separate React components, even though the rubric's behavioral phrasing correctly allows a single component with a data-driven branch.

_task_quality_flags.internet_with_live_origin: task.toml sets allow_internet=true. goal_0 anti-gaming guard names git-ops exfiltration as a zeroing trigger where applicable.

completeness_goals.goal: The submission is a genuine, functional implementation of the requested lightbox behavior, not a scoring artifact. REJECT if any of these gaming patterns are present: (a) a zero-edit or no-op submission, or a diff whose only content is a captured-tree banner such as a line reading '=== repo/ ===' with no real code change; (b) git-operations exfiltration that fabricates a passing state instead of editing source, e.g. 'git checkout main', 'git reset --hard origin/...', or restoring files from another ref; (c) a comment-only or whitespace-only diff that changes no executable behavior; (d) changes confined to temporary, scratch, or unmerged locations (e.g. /tmp, *.bak, untracked throwaway files) rather than the real component/hook source; (e) deleting, weakening, or short-circuiting existing tests to force a pass. The change must modify the actual application source under the TasksPane component tree and/or the segment-slot/lightbox handling code and produce the described runtime behavior.
completeness_goals.rationale: Floor to exclude non-genuine submissions. Calibrated against the lowest-reward real attempt (glm51, reward None) whose entire diff is preceded by a '=== repo/ ===' captured-tree banner and touches only the receiver hook — and against the three 0.1 model patches that edited real TasksPane source but did not complete the multi-turn behavior. Anti-gaming only; not scored.

completeness_goals.goal: Clicking a travel/segment video entry from the tasks pane opens it inside the originating shot's context — i.e. with the same in-context viewing experience the segment strip provides: forward/back navigation between sibling segments and visibility of the segment's constituent images — rather than an isolated standalone viewer that lacks that surrounding shot context. The detection of which task entries are segment videos must rely on a genuine, populated property of the task data model — for example the actual task-type field the model sets, or segment-specific parameters that real segment tasks carry — rather than a stub that always returns true/false, a hardcoded constant, or a check against a field/value that is never actually set; so that only real segment-video entries route into the in-context experience and other entries are unaffected. Any property that correctly and reliably distinguishes segment-video entries from other entries is acceptable.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn-0 verbatim: 'when I click into a travel segment from within the TasksPane, I want it to open within the context of the shot it's a part of - similar to how it would when i open via SegmentOutputStrip ... so that i can click back/forth to previous segments'. Also U5: 'it doesn't show the chevron, the buttons/links to the constituent images don't show/work ... opening it outside of the context of that shot.' The 'genuine task-type field' clause encodes the root-cause bug (detection predicate hit the wrong field) behaviorally without naming the identifier; the three real model patches that only partially wired this scored 0.1.

completeness_goals.goal: When the in-context viewer is opened for a segment, the correct shot context (the shot the segment belongs to, and the segment's identity/position within that shot) is actually carried through to the viewer so that the surrounding segments and their navigation are populated — not left empty. A passed-but-ignored context value, an always-undefined identifier, or context that is computed but never delivered to the viewer does NOT satisfy this: the viewer must receive a non-empty, correct shot/segment context derived from the clicked task.
completeness_goals.rationale: U5 debug logs show the failure mode the user reported: 'shotId: undefined ... segmentSlotsCount: 0' — the context arrived empty so chevrons/constituent images were blank. The user's Turn-0 goal ('click back/forth to previous segments') is only met if the shot/segment identity is correctly propagated to the receiver. Behavioral phrasing ('shot context carried through', 'non-empty') avoids naming the openSegmentSlot/shotId identifiers, which the user did not state verbatim.

completeness_goals.goal: Opening the in-context viewer from the tasks pane no longer briefly flashes the empty / no-video state before the real media appears: the viewer waits until the segment's video/media data is actually available before presenting it, so the user sees the populated media directly. A change that merely hides or styles the empty state without ensuring the real data is loaded first, or that introduces an unconditional delay, does NOT satisfy this — the gating must be on actual data availability.
completeness_goals.rationale: U7 verbatim (Turn 3 deliverable): 'when i clicked it it first showed the empty form - the one that shows when there's no video for a segment - before switching out to the proper media lightbox'. Multi-turn correction. Phrased behaviorally (wait for data availability) rather than naming the segmentSlots/pairDataByIndex effect.

completeness_goals.goal: Segment entries whose underlying shot position has been deleted/orphaned (the video still exists but the segment is no longer present on the shot's timeline) are handled with a distinct fallback: such entries open in the simple/standalone viewer instead of attempting the in-context shot experience, while genuinely connected segments still get the in-context experience. There must be a real, data-driven branch that distinguishes connected from orphaned segments — a single code path that sends ALL segments to the simple viewer, or ALL to the in-context viewer, does NOT satisfy this, and the distinction must reflect the actual current timeline/connection state rather than a hardcoded or always-true condition. Additionally, the two viewer modes must not interfere with each other: after viewing an orphaned segment in the simple viewer, clicking a connected segment must still successfully open it in the in-context experience (the simple/standalone viewer must not stay open and block the in-context one). Suppressing one mode entirely to avoid the conflict does NOT satisfy this — both must remain reachable in sequence.
completeness_goals.rationale: U9 verbatim: 'What about in cases where the video exists but it wouldn't actually display on the shot because that segment or position has been deleted? Can we fall back to the old-school viewer?' plus U10 'Let's do A' (chose the pre-check approach), U11 'Now it seems to be showing simple for all' (branch collapsed to one path), and the later stacking report: 'i first click into one that belongs to a shot and it enters it then i click into another one that doesn't belong ... then when i try to click back the other one it just doesn't work. However, when i close that and then click it does' (the open viewer blocked the next open). The 'distinct branch / not all-one-way' and 'must not stay open and block' clauses encode the U11 and stacking corrections behaviorally.

completeness_goals.goal: The 'enhance prompt' default behavior is corrected so it is no longer turned on unexpectedly. After the user clears a prompt, the enhance-prompt toggle is NOT silently flipped on; the default for the enhance-prompt setting is off everywhere EXCEPT the one batch-generation entry point, which keeps defaulting to on. The enhance-prompt state may only become on when genuinely persisting the user's own existing/saved preference. Critically, clearing the prompt must NOT destroy or reset that saved preference: if the user had previously enabled enhance-prompt, clearing the prompt must keep their setting intact rather than wiping it (which would later make it resolve to a default) — a fix that merely flips a fallback to off and ends up silently turning the user's own enabled preference off when they clear the prompt does NOT satisfy this. A fix that only changes one of the many places the default is set while leaving other paths still defaulting it on does NOT satisfy this — every default path for this setting must be brought into line (off by default, except batch generation). Merely commenting out, or hardcoding the toggle to a fixed value regardless of context, does NOT satisfy this.
completeness_goals.rationale: Second-half multi-turn arc (Turns 18-24). U18 verbatim: 'Why when I clear the prompt does enhance prompt get set to true?'; U19: 'It should always be set to false and nothing should ever set it to true ... find every instance of it ... It should only be true if it's persisting the user's existing settings.'; U21: 'Except on the batch generator, that's the one place you should default to true.' U19's 'It should only be true if it's persisting the user's existing settings' is the verbatim basis for the preserve-on-clear clause: the proximate bug wiped the saved preference on clear, so a fix that just flips the default off would silently lose a user's enabled setting. Behavioral phrasing avoids naming the enhancePrompt/enhance_prompt_enabled identifiers; the 'every default path' clause encodes the user's DRY/'tons of different places' complaint as the observable requirement (no path leaks an on-default). The thin U25 'make a .md doc' ask is a process/deliverable-format request, not a behavioral workspace outcome, so it is intentionally not given its own scored goal.

decomposition_notes: Evidence tier: model_only (golden.patch empty; 3 real model patches at reward 0.1 plus 1 banner-only patch at reward None). Target derived from instruction.md + the multi-turn user-sim trigger table + F2P tests. The session has two substantive multi-turn arcs: (1) the TasksPane lightbox in-context experience (Turns 0/U5/U7/U9/U10/U11 + stacking report), the headline deliverable; and (2) the enhance-prompt default cleanup (Turns 18-24), a 7-turn arc the user pushed on hard. Both are covered: arc 1 by goal_1..goal_4, arc 2 by goal_5. The manifest gates the enhance-prompt work only as a P2P_REGRESSION floor, but completeness must score every substantive ask, so it gets its own weighted goal here (weights deliberately do not mirror the manifest). The thin U25 'make a .md doc' request is a deliverable-format/process ask, not a behavioral workspace outcome, so it is folded into goal_5's rationale rather than scored as its own goal (per the no-process-goals rule). Weights rank by user-importance, NOT mirroring the F2P manifest weights: goal_1 (open in shot context + correct detection) is the headline Turn-0 ask and dominates at 0.40; goal_2 (context actually propagated non-empty) is the mechanism the user diagnosed via debug logs (0.20); goal_3 (no empty-form flash, U7, 0.13); goal_4 (orphaned-segment fallback + distinct branch + no stacking, U9/U11/stacking, 0.15); goal_5 (enhance-prompt defaults, U18-U21, 0.12). Sum of weighted goals = 0.40+0.20+0.13+0.15+0.12 = 1.00. All goal text is behavioral: no function/class/file/variable names, no line numbers, no exact-string literals — the user never named isSegmentVideoTask/openSegmentSlot/shotId/segmentSlots verbatim, so the rubric describes the effect (in-context navigation, correct detection, non-empty context, data-availability gating, connected-vs-orphaned branch) and would accept any of the structurally different valid implementations seen in the model patches (deep-link navigation vs in-place segment-slot lightbox). Each weighted goal embeds anti-cheating language (no stub/hardcoded/always-true/parsed-but-ignored/test-deletion).
